calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch.

Mr. Der reported that he received an email from Martin Klotivich, that he has withdrawn his objections and that Mr. Der forwarded the email to the probate examiner. The Court stated that the issue is whether it is a good idea to issue a joint check. Mr. Der stated that he received an email from Anthony Klotovich with percentages, that he e-filed a second supplement to the petition with the emails attached, that they have agreed on 43.5% for Anthony and 46.5% for Martin and that he will submit a courtesy copy and revised order. The Court asked Mr. Der if he had any objections to the $319.50 that the examiner raised, if it had been addressed to him. Mr. Der responded that it had not been sent to him. The Court granted the petition for accounting (subject to review of the order & review of the supplement).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Authority To Sell Estate Real Property
Off Calendar Jun-30-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for and with (petitioner) Pam Klotovich. Ernest Der for (petitioner) Debra Dolch.

Ms. Ebey reported that there have been no objections, that they have tried to work something out with the family but that there has been no response. Mr. Der reported that there have been no objections, that there has been no response from Robert Klotovich and that he will submit a proposed order. The Court granted the petition for authority to sell estate real property (subject to receipt/review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Apr-29-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for (Successor Administrator) Debra Dolch. Amanda Ebey for Pamela Klotovich. James Kaller for Martin Klotovich.

The Court asked if any additional information has been or can be gathered regarding the missing information and the alleged notes. Mr. Der reported that in regards to the notes there is nothing new, that no one seems to have information or documents, that he will work with the Probate Referee, that he has statements from Martin & Tony as to what the amounts owed on the notes were, that in regards to the Annuity they now have a date of death value and will work on an appraisal, that they now have information on stocks and will start marshaling them and that they have bank statements from Martin but still have questions. The Court stated that it will consider allowing the accounting from Martin so that Ms. Dolch can move forward. Mr. Der further stated that he has prepared a proposed distribution based on crude numbers to see what it would look like and that they have filed a petition for the sale of the family home which will go forward in June. Mr. Kaller reported that he has recently spoken with Martin, that Martin insists that he has turned everything over, that Martin has not agreed to mediation but is not opposed to it either and that he will not represent Martin individually if this does go to mediation. The Court stated that at best we are dealing with inadequate record keeping and at worst the hiding of evidence, that it does not see an advantage to keeping this matter pending, that it will allow but not approve the accounting, that there is concern regarding the conflicting statements regarding the existence of additional documentation and that to the extent the successor administrator needs information is hopeful that a mediated conference can occur. Mr. Der stated that the petition for the sale is in June and that there is still time for the parties to come together and agree to mediation but that there is no response from Robert and Anthony says "no way". The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2015 Continued To Apr-29-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2015 Continued To Apr-15-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for (administrator) Debra Dolch. James Kaller for (former administrator) Martin Klotovich. Pam Klotovich appeared without counsel, Amanda Ebey.

The court stated that we are here on the accounting of the former administrator, concerns were set forth in the examiner's notes regarding nothing to document starting values or the substantial loans and asked if there are any objections. Mr. Der stated that he understood the concerns, that a declaration was filed yesterday, that Ms. Dolch has not received any original bank statements, that she has only received 1 copy of a statement showing the ending balance prior to the transfer to her. Mr. Kaller reported that the current closing balance is zero and confirms the check to Ms. Dolch, that he should be able to get letters from the bank and that prior to the death of Mr. Klotovich there were promissory notes but does not know where they are. Ms. Klotovich stated that Tony removed the promissory notes from the house, was subsequently told by Martin that he had them in Montana but that now both brothers deny having them. Mr. Der reported that the father had kept records but that after his death the mother did not keep up with the record keeping, that he is investigating and trying to get information regarding the notes and that he plans to file a partial #1 inventory and appraisal once he gets it back from the referee. The accounting petition was continued to April 15, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are advised to file any objections they have and the current administrator is to determine what she needs to move forward.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition Of Pamela Ann Klotovich For Removal Of Administrator Martin L. Klotovich
Off Calendar Sep-23-2014 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-29-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for and with Pamela Klotovich. Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch (nominated Administrator). James Kaller for and with Martin Klotovich (appeared by telephone). Tony Klotovich in Propria Persona.

Martin stated that he does not want Mr. Kaller to continue representing him, that he faxed a resignation to the Probate Examiner and that it is his intent to resign. The Court informed Martin that an accounting is needed for the period for which he acted as Administrator and that he will need assistance from Counsel. Martin stated that he will move forward with getting an Attorney for approval of the accounting and that he has given all of the information to Mr. Kaller. The Court suggested Martin Klotovich and Mr. Kaller see it through to get the accounting on file, stated that the resignation needs to be filed with proof of service to all Parties, asked Martin to send/fax the resignation to Mr. Der for filing and directed Martin to keep Mr. Kaller informed. Ms. Ebey stated that she did not serve an amended proof of service, that the date was pre-set by the Court and that notice was served on August 15, 2014. Tony expressed his concerns. Status on filing of the accounting and substitution of attorney was set for November 18, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting is to be filed by November 14, 2014. Estate assets are to be turned over within 10 days. The Petition for Removal of Administrator was taken off calendar. The Petition for Letters of Administration was taken under submission pending receipt of the resignation and a proposed order appointing Ms. Dolch with limited powers and bond at $450,000.00. Mr. Der is to prepare the proposed order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-05-2014 Continued To Sep-23-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Special Letters Of Administration
Off Calendar Aug-05-2014 Continued To Sep-23-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Kaller for and with (administrator) Martin Klotovich. Amanda Ebey for and with Pamela Klotovich. Tony Klotovich, (brother) in Propria Persona.

The court raised concern about Mr. Klotovich having picked up the will on January 10, 2013 and filing an intestate petition on January 17, 2013. Martin Klotovich explained that he advised his attorney of having found the will and that his attorney told him that is was not necessary to file the will because distribution would be the same intestate. The court expressed further concern about the declarations regarding the will being filed one year later, not having an inventory, stated that this matter calls for appointment of an independent fiduciary and asked the parties to step out the courtroom and discuss the matter. Upon returning Mr. Kaller stated that they have agreed to have Debra Dolch take over and asked how to proceed with the process. The court directed that Ms. Ebey could amend her petition to have Ms. Dolch serve with bond, to file a consent by Ms. Dolch, to do publication and to file the original will. Discussion was held regarding expenses, bank accounts, investment accounts, safe deposit box, savings bonds, Pamela living in the home, Martin's attempt for equalization, personal loans and whether any records are available. The court suggested that the parties exchange documents and try and locate records regarding the loans. The court suspends Martin Klotovich's powers of administrator, revoked his letters of administration and suggested that he sign a resignation. The Petition for letters of special administration, petition for letters of administration with will annexed and petition for removal of administrator Martin Klotivich were continued to September 23, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
